title: cni.lock file not relocated when XDG_DATA_HOME and XDG_CONFIG_DIR are set
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running as a user with home dir /tmp and with XDG_DATA_HOME=/foo XDG_CONFIG_DIR=/foo, /tmp/.config/cni/net.d/cni.lock
still gets created. 

Setting XDG_DATA_DIR=/foo has no effect. 

I have to use the "--network-config-dir=/foo" flag to write cni.local as /foo/cni.lock.

Is there a list of podman's XDG variables somewhere?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. XDG_DATA_HOME=/foo XDG_CONFIG_DIR=/foo podman system migrate

**Describe the results you received:**

/tmp/.config/cni/net.d/cni.lock

**Describe the results you expected:**

/foo/.config/cni/cni.lock

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I was unable to install btrfs-devel using dnf on this RHEL 8 system. Btrfs is necessary to compile the latest podman release.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

No. Yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
In the output of podman config
```
  configFile: /tmp/.config/containers/storage.conf
```
I.e., configFile is still for some purposes using $HOME (/tmp) as the location of the .config directory. There should be a way to relocate the entire .config directory.

Setting XDG_CONFIG_HOME relocates neither the .config nor .local directories, at least not completely. Both directories still appear in $HOME, and both contain files. Even if I set all the XDG variables, the cni.lock file does not get relocated
```
TMPDIR=/bs-grafana/var/tmp XDG_CONFIG_DIR=/bs-grafana XDG_DATA_HOME=/bs-grafana XDG_STATE_HOME=/bs-grafana XDG_DATA_DIRS=/bs-grafana XDG_CONFIG_DIRS=/bs-grafana XDG_CACHE_HOME=/bs-grafana XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/bs-grafana podman run
```
